profile.name: James
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner; tends to drive meeting agenda and follow-ups
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable with retirement accounts and basic investing concepts, asks about tax impact and tradeoffs, but doesn’t always track every account detail day-to-day
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate risk taker in practice: willing to stay invested and accept normal market swings if the plan is clear; gets uneasy when positions feel 'complicated' (like alternatives) or when outcomes aren’t framed in probabilities
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; uses numbers when he has them, but will hedge with 'I think' or 'roughly' when recalling details. Occasionally interrupts to clarify terminology (taxable vs. retirement, interest rate vs. payment). Uses quick summaries like 'so the big buckets are…' and asks for next steps.
emotional_tendencies: mild anxiety about missing something important
relief when there’s a clear checklist
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and checklist-driven; prefers to compare 2–3 options, then decide quickly once he understands the downside
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting: open to recommendations but wants the rationale and fees/constraints explained plainly
internal_biases: anchoring to current monthly expenses as the 'baseline'
status quo bias around existing retirement setup
hidden_concerns: worries about being in a higher tax bracket and making a mistake that creates an unexpected tax bill
concerned about balancing retirement saving with supporting three kids without feeling stretched

profile.name: Susan
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; primary owner of the taxable brokerage account and tends to focus on stability and near-term cash flow
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands budgeting and the idea of diversification, but mixes up account labels and tax terms at times; prefers plain-English explanations over jargon
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but more loss-averse than James: reacts more strongly to short-term declines and prefers smoother returns; may favor 'income' framing and asks whether something is 'safe' even when she accepts some risk
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; speaks in round numbers and ranges ('about', 'around'). Uses fillers ('um', 'you know') and occasionally misstates gross vs. net or balance vs. payment, then self-corrects. Often pauses to let James answer first, but will jump in if something affects the brokerage account or household spending.
emotional_tendencies: stress when conversations feel too technical
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants to hear the advisor’s recommendation, then sanity-check it against household comfort and liquidity before agreeing
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Moderate: trusts professionalism but needs time and repetition to feel confident; appreciates transparency and written summaries
internal_biases: loss aversion (focuses on avoiding big drawdowns)
hidden_concerns: concerned about having enough accessible funds for surprises despite investing
quiet worry about being behind on retirement given being closer to retirement age than James
